content.full_text:
CITY OF OCEANSIDE

DANIEL S. HENTSCHKE
INTERIM CITY ATTORNEY

DEBRA E. CORBETT
ASSISTANT CITY ATTORNEY

FRANK A. BALISTRIERI
DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY

JOSEPH L. STINE
DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY

ELIAS J. SANCHEZ
LEGAL ASSISTANT

MICHAEL W. BARRETT
LAW CLERK

September 18, 1991

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804

Re:  Request for Formal Written Advice

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

     This request for formal written advice is made pursuant to
Government Code § 83114(b) and 2 Cal. Code of Regs. § 18329 on
behalf of John Mamaux, City Manager for the City of Oceanside.  Mr.
Mamaux has requested that I make this request on his behalf.
Correspondence with respect to this request may be directed to me
at the City Attorney's Office, City of Oceanside, 300 North Hill
Street, Oceanside, CA 92054.

     The facts related to the request are as follows:

     The City of Oceanside is the owner of a municipal golf
course.  The city has a contract with a company called
American Golf for the operation of the golf course.  The
city is considering raising the green fees for play at
the municipal golf course.  In addition, there has been
discussion of a possible renegotiation of the contract
between the city and American Golf.  The renegotiation of
the contract is related to the proposed increase in green
fees, although the increase in green fees may occur
without amendment to the contract for golf course
operation.  Mr. Mamaux is the City Manager for the City
of Oceanside.  In this capacity he is responsible for
general supervision of all city department heads,
including the head of the recreation department.  In
addition, he is responsible for making recommendations to
the City Council regarding financial matters.  Currently,
the City of Oceanside is experiencing a severe budget
crisis.  In order to help alleviate the crisis a
suggestion has been made to increase the green fees at
the municipal golf course.  It has also been proposed
that the operation contract be revised in order to
provide greater revenue to the City.

300 NORTH HILL STREET • OCEANSIDE, CA 92054 • FAX 619-966-4436 • PHONE 619-966-4444

Scott Hallabrin
September 18, 1991
Page 2

Mr. Mamaux and his wife are owners of a family "golf"
membership in a private country club located in the city
of Oceanside called the El Camino Country Club.  The
membership was originally purchased by Mr. Mamaux's wife
in 1958 while she was still a single person.  After her
marriage to Mr. Mamaux in 1965, her individual membership
in the country club was converted to a family membership.
The membership was originally purchased for approximately
$750.00.  Currently the membership has a sale value of
approximately $10,000.00.  Neither Mr. Mamaux nor his
wife have any intention of selling the membership.
Membership in the El Camino Country Club confers certain
membership privileges, but does not represent any
ownership in the club or in the land company which owns
the land upon which the club is located.  Mr. Mamaux is
currently serving as a member of the Board of Directors
of the Country Club.

The El Camino Country Club is a private club.  Non-
members are not permitted to play golf at the country
club except as a guest of a member, or during certain
special "tournament" events.  The Oceanside Municipal
Golf Course is a public golf course open to any person
who pays the required fees and complies with the rules
and regulations of the course.  The fees for play at the
municipal golf course are paid on a pay-as-you-play
basis.

The question presented is whether Mr. Mamaux may make,
participate in making, or use his position as City Manager to
influence a decision of the City Council regarding: (1) the fee
increase for the municipal golf course, or (2) the renegotiation of
the contract with American Golf for the operation of the municipal
golf course, in light of his "investment" in the El Camino Country
Club.

In an advice letter dated November 30, 1990, to Betsy Strauss,
City Attorney for the City of Fairfield [File No. I-90-654] you
indicated that a membership interest with a fair market value of
$1,000.00 or more in a country club which is a business entity is
an "investment" in the business entity if the membership can be
resold at a profit or a loss.  The "golf" membership is not
generally a source of income.  However, it can be sold by its owner
directly to a purchaser.  It is personal rather than real property.
Government Code Section 87103 provides that a local official has a
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision will have a material financial effect,
distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on any
business entity in which the official has a direct or indirect
investment worth $1,000.00 or more, or in which the official is a
director or holds any position of management.

Scott Hallabrin
September 18, 1991
Page 3

The El Camino Country Club is not directly involved in the
decision to increase fees at the municipal golf course, nor in the
decisions relating to the renegotiation of the contract with
American Golf. In order to conclude that the El Camino Country
Club would be indirectly involved in the decision, it would appear
that a nexus between the increased fees for play at the municipal
golf course and the demand for memberships sold by the country club
would have to be found. The manager of the country club indicated
in a telephone interview that the demand for memberships in the
country club and the availability of those memberships is entirely
independent of the fees for golf at the municipal course. The
Country Club has a set limit of 525 golf memberships. According to
the manager, the memberships fluctuate between 500 and 510 members.
The manager indicates that golf memberships have never reached the
525 limit. The club has 509 members as of the date of this letter.
The club currently sells new memberships for $12,500.00. Members
are free to sell their own memberships at whatever price the member
sets without approval or interference from the club. The price
received from the sale of an individual's membership is retained by
the seller. The club retains a perpetual list of members who are
offering their memberships for sale. Prospective club members may
purchase a membership from the club or from a member on the list.
According to the manager, sales of memberships fluctuates only when
major renovations are made to the club's course, or when there is
an announced price increase for memberships sold by the club.

If you need any further information with respect to this
matter, please let me know.

Very truly yours,

DANIEL S. HENTSCHKE
Interim City Attorney

cc: John Mamaux
